tools_used: CourtListener MCP opinion search and endpoint access
helpful: The provisioned petition supplied the question, factual posture, and vehicle analysis.
The statpack supplied fee-class, circuit, relist, and CVSG base rates.
CourtListener MCP showed how the established warrant-hearsay doctrine appears in case law.
gaps: The submitted brief in opposition was listed on the snapshot but was not provisioned as text.
The ranged corpus query could not reach the remote because DNS resolution failed.
notes: The corpus-query CLI was invoked but returned no priors or ranged-read statistics. The prediction degraded cleanly to the provisioned inputs, committed base rates, and sanctioned MCP research.
